content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMrVIISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660
• Fax (916) 322-0886
May 1, 1998
Fred Huebscher
President
California Democratic Alliance
2912 Old Bennett Ridge Road
Santa Rosa, California 95404
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-98-098
Dear Mr. Huebscher:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the slate mailer provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
Does section 84305.5(a)(2) require a joint slate mailer produced by two slate mailer
organizations to have two disclaimers for each slate mailer organization or one joint disclaimer
that lists the names ofboth slate mailer organizations?
CONCLUSION
Section 84305.5(a)(2) requires the disclaimer on a joint slate mailer to list the name of the
slate mailer organization that paid the largest portion of expenditures attributable to the
designing, printing, and posting of the mailer.
FACTS
California Democratic Alliance is a slate mailer organization (ID #590 1 1). California
Democratic Alliance intends to produce a joint slate mailer with Parents and Teachers For A
Better California, which is also a slate mailer organization (ID #5960 16). Both organizations
1 Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-98-098
Page 2
will be supporting every candidate and ballot measure endorsed in the slate mailer. You intend
to produce this joint slate mailer for the upcoming June 2, 1998, primary election.
The size of
the slate mailer will be 8 V2 x 11 folded in half. During a telephone conversation on April 24,
1998, you indicated that the slate mailer organizations will not be contributing equally toward the
payment ofthe joint slate mailer.
ANALYSIS
The Act requires candidates and committees that send over 200 substantially similar
pieces of mail to include the name and address of the sender on each mailing. (Sections 82041.5,
84305.) Section 84305.5 imposes additional requirements on slate mailer organizations that send
slate mailers. A “slate mailer” means over 200 substantially similar pieces of mail that support
or oppose four or more candidates or ballot measures. (Sections 82041.5, 82048.3.) A “slate
mailer organization” is a person that receives $500 or more per calendar year to produce slate
mailers. (Section 82048.4.) A slate mailer must include the name and address of the slate mailer
organization that produced the slate mailer. (Section 84305.5(a)(1).) In addition, the mailer
must include a notice that provides:
NOTICE TO VOTERS
THIS DOCUMENT WAS PREPARED BY (name of slate mailer
organization or committee primarily formed to support or oppose
one or more ballot measures), NOT AN OFFICIAL POLITICAL
PARTY ORGANIZATION. Appearance in this mailer does not
necessarily imply endorsement of others appearing in this mailer,
nor does it imply endorsement of, or opposition to, any issues set
forth in this mailer. Appearance is paid for and authorized by each
candidate and ballot measure which is designated by an
2 Section 84305.5 was recently amended by Proposition 208. However, the provisions of Proposition 208
are presently subject to a preliminary injunction. (California Prolfe Council Political Action Committee v. Scully
(E.D. Cal. 1998) ---- F. Supp. ----, No. CWS-96-l965 LICK/DAD, 1998 WL 7173.) The Proposition 208 version of
section 84305.5(a)(2) requires a slate mailer to include the following notice:
NOTICE TO VOTERS
THIS DOCUMENT WAS PREPARED BY (name of slate mailer
organization or committee primarily formed to support or oppose one or more
ballot measures), NOT AN OFFICIAL POLITICAL PARTY
ORGANIZATION. All candidates and ballot measures designated by $$$ have
paid for their listing in this mailer. A listing in this mailer does not necessarily
imply endorsement of other candidates or measures listed in this mailer.


File No. A-98-098
Page 3
The notice must be printed as follows: at the top or bottom of the front side or surface of
at least one insert or at the top or bottom of one side or surface of a postcard or other self-mailer;
in at least 8-point roman boldface type; in a color or print that contrasts with the background so
as to be easily legible; and in a printed or drawn box set apart from any other printed matter.
3
(Section 84305.5(a)(2).)
You would like to know whether a slate mailer produced by two slate mailer
organizations must include two disclaimers or a joint disclaimer listing both slate mailer
organizations.
The Commission has not rendered formal advice regarding joint slate mailers. However,
we have addressed a similar issue in the context of section 84305, which imposes sender
identification requirements on candidates and committees that send mass mailings. Regulation
18435 interprets section 84305 and provides that the “sender” of a mass mailing is the candidate
or committee thatpaysfor the largestportion of expenditures attributable to the designing,
printing, and posting of the mailing. (Regulation 18435(b).) Thus, when multiple candidates
send a mailer, the candidate who paid the largest amount towards the production ofthe mailer is
the one who is identified on the mailer. (Sher Advice Letter, No. A-96-036.)
We conclude that regulation 18435(b) may be used, by analogy, to determine the
identification requirements applicable to joint slate mailers. One purpose behind section 84305.5
is to inform voters that a slate mailer was produced by an organization that is not an official
political party organization. (Los Angeles Taxpayers Alliance v. Fair Political Practices Comm.
(1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 1214, 1221.) This public policy is served by listing the name of one slate
mailer organization on the slate mailer.
Accordingly, a joint slate mailer produced by two slate mailer organizations must include
the name and address of the slate mailer organization that paid the largest portion of expenditures
attributable to the designing, printing and posting of the mailer. Further, the disclaimer required
by section 84305.5(a)(2) must list the slate mailer organization that paid the largest portion of
expenditures attributable to the designing, printing and posting of the mailer.
Please note that prior to the passage of Proposition 208, the Legislature amended section
84305.5 to provide:
Proposition 208 amended section 84305.5 and required a notice to be printed as follows: at the top of
each side or surface of a slate mailer or at the top of each side or surface of a postcard or other self-mailer; in at least
8-point roman boldface type; in a color or print that contrasts with the background so as to be easily legible; and in a
printed or drawn box set apart from any other printed matter. (Section 84305.5(a)(2).)


File No. A-98-098
Page 4
“Any candidate endorsement appearing in the slate mailer that
differs from the official endorsement of the political party which
the mailer appears by representation or indicia to represent is
accompanied, immediately below the name, by the party
designation of the candidate, in no less than 9-point roman type
which shall be in a color or print that contrasts with the
background so as to be easily legible, the following notice: THIS
IS NOT TIlE POSITION OF THE (political party which the
mailer appears by representation or indicia to represent) PARTY.”
(Section 84305.5(a)(6), see Chapter 893, Stats. 1996.)
During the period that the preliminary injunction is in effect, slate mailer organizations
must comply with section 84305.5(a)(6).
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Julia Butcher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does section 84305.5(a)(2) require a joint slate mailer produced by two slate mailer
organizations to have two disclaimers for each slate mailer organization or one joint disclaimer
that lists the names ofboth slate mailer organizations?

CONCLUSION: Section 84305.5(a)(2) requires the disclaimer on a joint slate mailer to list the name of the
slate mailer organization that paid the largest portion of expenditures attributable to the
designing, printing, and posting of the mailer.
